revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Kōtai Shrine (Shijōōji, Nara)]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.comment:
[[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 皇大神社 |画像 = [[画像:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|270px]]
皇大神社 |所在地 = [[奈良県]]奈良市…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 皇大神社 |画像 = [[画像:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|270px]]
皇大神社 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[平松町]] |祭神 = [[天照大神|大日霊貴命]]
[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]
[[イワナガヒメ|磐長媛命]] |社格 = 旧村社、神饌幣帛料供進社{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = 三間社流造銅板葺{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = [[10月10日]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city }} '''皇大神社'''（こうたいじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[平松町]]にある神社。 == 祭神 == * [[天照大神|大日霊貴命]] * [[天児屋命|天児屋根命]] * [[イワナガヒメ|磐長媛命]] == 境内 == === 本殿 === 三間社流造、銅板葺{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。[[春日大社]]摂社水谷神社の旧本殿の移しで、平松惣代森田平太郎が春日大社の御殿木係を務めていた縁で賜ったと伝わる{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 === 境内社 === * 厳島神社 - [[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]] * 住吉神社 - [[住吉三神|上筒男命]]外 本殿の両脇に鎮座し、いずれも朱塗の春日造檜皮葺の小祠{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 === 垂仁天皇陵陪塚跡 === 境内森の中に[[安山岩]]の巨石があり、[[宝来山古墳|垂仁天皇陵]]の陪塚の跡と伝わる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。正月には[[注連縄]]が張られる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 === 観音堂 === 境内に接して西に観音堂と呼ばれる場所があり、古い瓦片などが見つかっている{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 ==社宝== ===神楽鈴=== 「[[慶長]]十年([[1605年]])[[3月16日 (旧暦)|三月十六日]]ヒラマツ宇衛門」の陰刻銘がある{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 == ギャラリー == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|本殿 File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|二の鳥居 File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|右狛犬 File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|左狛犬 File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|右境内社 File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|左境内社 == 出典 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA)}} {{osm box|n|2463618290}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 神社}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 8 建築|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 建築}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうたいしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:春日移し|摂]]

{{神社 |名称 = 皇大神社 |画像 = [[画像:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|270px]]
皇大神社 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[平松町]] |祭神 = [[天照大神|大日霊貴命]]
[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]
[[イワナガヒメ|磐長媛命]] |社格 = 旧村社、神饌幣帛料供進社{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = 三間社流造銅板葺{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = [[10月10日]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city }} '''皇大神社'''（こうたいじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[平松町]]にある神社。 == 祭神 == * [[天照大神|大日霊貴命]] * [[天児屋命|天児屋根命]] * [[イワナガヒメ|磐長媛命]] == 境内 == === 本殿 === 三間社流造、銅板葺{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。[[春日大社]]摂社水谷神社の旧本殿の移しで、平松惣代森田平太郎が春日大社の御殿木係を務めていた縁で賜ったと伝わる{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 === 境内社 === * 厳島神社 - [[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]] * 住吉神社 - [[住吉三神|上筒男命]]外 本殿の両脇に鎮座し、いずれも朱塗の春日造檜皮葺の小祠{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 === 垂仁天皇陵陪塚跡 === 境内森の中に[[安山岩]]の巨石があり、[[宝来山古墳|垂仁天皇陵]]の陪塚の跡と伝わる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。正月には[[注連縄]]が張られる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 === 観音堂 === 境内に接して西に観音堂と呼ばれる場所があり、古い瓦片などが見つかっている{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 ==社宝== ===神楽鈴=== 「[[慶長]]十年([[1605年]])[[3月16日 (旧暦)|三月十六日]]ヒラマツ宇衛門」の陰刻銘がある{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 == ギャラリー == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|本殿 File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|二の鳥居 File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|右狛犬 File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|左狛犬 File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|右境内社 File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|左境内社 == 出典 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA)}} {{osm box|n|2463618290}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 神社}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 8 建築|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 建築}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうたいしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:春日移し|摂]] [[Category:神明神社]]

[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]
[[イワナガヒメ|磐長媛命]] |社格 = 旧村社、神饌幣帛料供進社{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = 三間社流造銅板葺{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = [[10月10日]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city }} '''皇大神社'''（こうたいじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]平松町にある神社。 == 祭神 == * [[天照大神|大日霊貴命]] * [[天児屋命|天児屋根命]] * [[イワナガヒメ|磐長媛命]] == 境内 == === 本殿 === 三間社流造、銅板葺{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。[[春日大社]]摂社水谷神社の旧本殿の移しで、平松惣代森田平太郎が春日大社の御殿木係を務めていた縁で賜ったと伝わる{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 === 境内社 === * 厳島神社 - [[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]] * 住吉神社 - [[住吉三神|上筒男命]]外 本殿の両脇に鎮座し、いずれも朱塗の春日造檜皮葺の小祠{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 === 垂仁天皇陵陪塚跡 === 境内森の中に[[安山岩]]の巨石があり、[[宝来山古墳|垂仁天皇陵]]の陪塚の跡と伝わる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。正月には[[注連縄]]が張られる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 === 観音堂 === 境内に接して西に観音堂と呼ばれる場所があり、古い瓦片などが見つかっている{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 ==社宝== ===神楽鈴=== 「[[慶長]]十年([[1605年]])[[3月16日 (旧暦)|三月十六日]]ヒラマツ宇衛門」の陰刻銘がある{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 == ギャラリー == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|本殿 File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|二の鳥居 File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|右狛犬 File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|左狛犬 File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|右境内社 File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|左境内社 == 出典 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA)}} {{osm box|n|2462801468}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 神社}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 8 建築|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 建築}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうたいしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:春日移し|摂]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Nihongo|'''Kōtai Shrine'''|皇大神社|Kōtai jinja}} is a name used by some [[Shinmei shrines]] == Main Kōtai Shrine == * {{ill|Amaterasu Kōtai Shrine (Itō)|ja|天照皇大神社 (伊東市)}} – Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Itō|ja|伊東市}} Shibamachi: Kusumi Shrine * {{ill|Kōtai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|lt=Kotai Shrine (Fukuchiyama)}} – Kyoto Prefecture, {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}} Ōe-chō Naikū: Motoise Kōdaijingū * {{ill|..."

{{Nihongo|'''Kōtai Shrine'''|皇大神社|Kōtai jinja}} is a name used by some [[Shinmei shrines]]
== Main Kōtai Shrine ==
* {{ill|Amaterasu Kōtai Shrine (Itō)|ja|天照皇大神社 (伊東市)}} – Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Itō|ja|伊東市}} Shibamachi: Kusumi Shrine * {{ill|Kōtai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|lt=Kotai Shrine (Fukuchiyama)}} – Kyoto Prefecture, {{ill|Fukuchiyama|ja|福知山市}} Ōe-chō Naikū: Motoise Kōdaijingū * {{ill|Obama Kōtai Shrine|ja|小浜皇大神社}} – Hyōgo Prefecture, {{ill|Takarazuka|ja|宝塚市}} Obama * {{ill|Kōtai Shrine (Hiramatsuchō, Nara)|ja|皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)|lt=Kōtai Shrine (Hiramatsuchō, Nara)}} – Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Hiramatsuchō * {{ill|Kōtai Shrine (Shijōōji, Nara)|ja|皇大神社 (奈良市四条大路)|lt=Kōtai Shrine (Shijōōji, Nara)}} – Nara Prefecture, Nara City Shijōōji

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) |画像 = {{ill|File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|ja|画像:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|lt=270px|lt_ja=270px}}
皇大神社 |所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} Heimamatsuchō |祭神 = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Great Sun Spirit|lt_ja=大日霊貴命}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|lt_ja=天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|イワナガヒメ|lt=Iwanagahime|lt_ja=磐長媛命}} |社格 = Former village shrine; offerings of sacred food, cloth, and ritual money{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = Built in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city }} '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Heimamatsuchō, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Great Sun Spirit|lt_ja=大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|lt_ja=天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|イワナガヒメ|lt=Iwanagahime|lt_ja=磐長媛命}} == Shrine Grounds == === Main Hall === Constructed in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}}, bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|lt=Ichikishimahime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder {{ill|Andesite|ja|安山岩}}, which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “{{ill|Keicho|ja|慶長}} 10th year ({{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Main Hall File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Secondary Torii File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Sub-shrine File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Sub-shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA)}} {{osm box|n|2462801468}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Kasuga Utsushi]] [[Category:Shinmei Shrines]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Kasuga Utsushi]] [[Category:Shinmei Shrines]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Iwanaga Faith]]{{Iwanaga Faith}}[[Category:Iwanaga Faith]] [[Category:Qq]][[ja:皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)]] [[ja:皇大神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) |画像 = {{ill|File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|ja|画像:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|lt=270px|lt_ja=270px}}
皇大神社 |所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} Heimamatsuchō |祭神 = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Great Sun Spirit|lt_ja=大日霊貴命}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|lt_ja=天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|イワナガヒメ|lt=Iwanagahime|lt_ja=磐長媛命}} |社格 = Former village shrine; offerings of sacred food, cloth, and ritual money{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = Built in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city }} '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Heimamatsuchō, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Great Sun Spirit|lt_ja=大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|lt_ja=天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|イワナガヒメ|lt=Iwanagahime|lt_ja=磐長媛命}} == Shrine Grounds == === Main Hall === Constructed in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}}, bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|lt=Ichikishimahime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder {{ill|Andesite|ja|安山岩}}, which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “{{ill|Keicho|ja|慶長}} 10th year ({{ill|1605|ja|1605th year }}) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Main Hall File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Secondary Torii File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Sub-shrine File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Sub-shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA)}} {{osm box|n|2462801468}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Kasuga Utsushi]] [[Category:Shinmei Shrines]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Kasuga Utsushi]] [[Category:Shinmei Shrines]] {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime|lt_ja=磐長媛命}} |社格 = Former village shrine; offerings of sacred food, cloth, and ritual money{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = Built in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city }} '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Heimamatsuchō, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Great Sun Spirit|lt_ja=大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|lt_ja=天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime|lt_ja=磐長媛命}} == Shrine Grounds == === Main Hall === Constructed in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}}, bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|lt=Ichikishimahime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder {{ill|Andesite|ja|安山岩}}, which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “{{ill|Keicho|ja|慶長}} 10th year ({{ill|1605|ja|1605th year }}) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Main Hall File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Secondary Torii File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Sub-shrine File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Sub-shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA)}} {{osm box|n|2462801468}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Kasuga Utsushi]] [[Category:Shinmei revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 皇大神社 |画像 = [[画像:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|270px]]
皇大神社 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]平松町 |祭神 = [[天照大神|大日霊貴命]]
[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]
[[イワナガヒメ|磐長媛命]] |社格 = 旧村社、神饌幣帛料供進社{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = 三間社流造銅板葺{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = [[10月10日]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city }} '''皇大神社'''（こうたいじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]平松町にある神社。 == 祭神 == * [[天照大神|大日霊貴命]] * [[天児屋命|天児屋根命]] * [[イワナガヒメ|磐長媛命]] == 境内 == === 本殿 === 三間社流造、銅板葺{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。[[春日大社]]摂社水谷神社の旧本殿の移しで、平松惣代森田平太郎が春日大社の御殿木係を務めていた縁で賜ったと伝わる{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 === 境内社 === * 厳島神社 - [[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]] * 住吉神社 - [[住吉三神|上筒男命]]外 本殿の両脇に鎮座し、いずれも朱塗の春日造檜皮葺の小祠{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 === 垂仁天皇陵陪塚跡 === 境内森の中に[[安山岩]]の巨石があり、[[宝来山古墳|垂仁天皇陵]]の陪塚の跡と伝わる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。正月には[[注連縄]]が張られる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 === 観音堂 === 境内に接して西に観音堂と呼ばれる場所があり、古い瓦片などが見つかっている{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 ==社宝== ===神楽鈴=== 「[[慶長]]十年([[1605年]])[[3月16日 (旧暦)|三月十六日]]ヒラマツ宇衛門」の陰刻銘がある{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}。 == ギャラリー == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|本殿 File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|二の鳥居 File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|右狛犬 File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|左狛犬 File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|右境内社 File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|左境内社 == 出典 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA)}} {{osm box|n|2462801468}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 神社}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 8 建築|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 建築}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうたいしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:春日移し|摂]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

[[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] |社格 = Former Village shrine; offerings of sacred food, cloth, and ritual money{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = Built in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = [[:en:October 10|October 10]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city }} '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Heimamatsuchō, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. == Deities == * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] == Shrine Grounds == === Main Hall === Constructed in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]], bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsutsunoo|qq=}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “[[:en:Keichō|Keicho]] 10th year ([[:en:1605|1605]]) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 16 (Old Calendar)|qq=}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] |社格 = Former Village shrine; offerings of sacred food, cloth, and ritual money{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = Built in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = [[:en:October 10|October 10]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city }} '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Heimamatsuchō, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. == Deities == * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] == Shrine Grounds == === Main Hall === Constructed in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]], bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsutsunoo|qq=}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “[[:en:Keichō|Keicho]] 10th year ([[:en:1605|1605]]) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 16 (Old Calendar)|qq=}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Main Hall File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Secondary Torii File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Sub-shrine File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Sub-shrine == References == It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]], bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsutsunoo|qq=}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “[[:en:Keichō|Keicho]] 10th year ([[:en:1605|1605]]) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 16 (Old Calendar)|qq=}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Main Hall File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Secondary Torii File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Sub-shrine File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Sub-shrine == References == [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] |社格 = Former Village shrine; offerings of sacred food, cloth, and ritual money{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = Built in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = [[:en:October 10|October 10]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city }} '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Heimamatsuchō, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. == Deities == * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] == Shrine Grounds == === Main Hall === Constructed in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]], bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsutsunoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “[[:en:Keichō|Keicho]] 10th year ([[:en:1605|1605]]) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 16 (Old Calendar)|qq=}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Main Hall File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Secondary Torii File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Sub-shrine File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Sub-shrine == References == [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] |社格 = Former Village shrine; offerings of sacred food, cloth, and ritual money{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = Built in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = [[:en:October 10|October 10]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city }} '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Heimamatsuchō, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. == Deities == * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] == Shrine Grounds == === Main Hall === Constructed in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]], bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsutsunoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “[[:en:Keichō|Keicho]] 10th year ([[:en:1605|1605]]) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 16 (Old Calendar)|qq=}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Main Hall File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Secondary Torii File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Sub-shrine File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Sub-shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA)}} {{osm box|n|2462801468}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)|version=82722734|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)]] [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] |社格 = Former Village shrine; offerings of sacred food, cloth, and ritual money{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = Built in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = [[:en:October 10|October 10]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city |image = File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg |location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.6791806|N|135.7792028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Heimamatsuchō, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. == Deities == * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] == Shrine Grounds == === Main Hall === Constructed in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]], bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsutsunoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “[[:en:Keichō|Keicho]] 10th year ([[:en:1605|1605]]) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 16 (Old Calendar)|qq=}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja [[sn:commons:Category:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, Nara)]][[ja:皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48754918]] Wikidata name: Kotai Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称 = '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) |画像 = {{ill|File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|ja|File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|lt=270px|lt_ja=270px|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
皇大神社 |所在地 = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] Heimamatsuchō |祭神 = [[:en:Amaterasu|Great Sun Spirit]]
[[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] |社格 = Former Village shrine; offerings of sacred food, cloth, and ritual money{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = Built in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = [[:en:October 10|October 10]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city |image = File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg |location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.6791806|N|135.7792028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Heimamatsuchō, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. == Deities == * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] == Shrine Grounds == === Main Hall === Constructed in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]], bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsutsunoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “[[:en:Keichō|Keicho]] 10th year ([[:en:1605|1605]]) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 16 (Old Calendar)|qq=|1=16th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839338}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Main Hall File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Secondary Torii File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Sub-shrine File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Sub-shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, [[sn:commons:Category:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, Nara)]][[ja:皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48754918]] Wikidata name: Kotai Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称 = '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) |画像 = {{ill|File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|ja|File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|lt=270px|lt_ja=270px|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
皇大神社 |所在地 = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] Heimamatsuchō |祭神 = [[:en:Amaterasu|Great Sun Spirit]]
[[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] |社格 = Former Village shrine; offerings of sacred food, cloth, and ritual money{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = Built in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = [[:en:October 10|October 10]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city |image = File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg |location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.6791806|N|135.7792028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Heimamatsuchō, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. == Deities == * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] == Shrine Grounds == === Main Hall === Constructed in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]], bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “[[:en:Keichō|Keicho]] 10th year ([[:en:1605|1605]]) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 16 (Old Calendar)|1=16th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839338}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Main Hall File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Secondary Torii File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Sub-shrine File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Sub-shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA)}} {{osm box|n|2462801468}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)|version=82722734|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)]] It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]], bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “[[:en:Keichō|Keicho]] 10th year ([[:en:1605|1605]]) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 16 (Old Calendar)|1=16th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839338}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Main Hall File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Secondary Torii File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Sub-shrine File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Sub-shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, [[sn:commons:Category:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, Nara)]][[ja:皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48754918]] Wikidata name: Kotai Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称 = '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) |画像 = [[Category:Pages with files linked by ill]]{{ill|File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|ja|File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|lt=270px|lt_ja=270px|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
皇大神社 |所在地 = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] Heimamatsuchō |祭神 = [[:en:Amaterasu|Great Sun Spirit]]
[[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] |社格 = Former Village shrine; offerings of sacred food, cloth, and ritual money{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = Built in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = [[:en:October 10|October 10]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city |image = File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg |location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.6791806|N|135.7792028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Heimamatsuchō, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. == Deities == * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] == Shrine Grounds == === Main Hall === Constructed in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]], bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “[[:en:Keichō|Keicho]] 10th year ([[:en:1605|1605]]) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 16 (Old Calendar)|1=16th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839338}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Main Hall File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Secondary Torii File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Sub-shrine File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Sub-shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA)}} {{osm box|n|2462801468}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)|version=82722734|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)]] It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]], bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “[[:en:Keichō|Keicho]] 10th year ([[:en:1605|1605]]) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 16 (Old Calendar)|1=16th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “[[:en:Keichō|Keicho]] 10th year ([[:en:1605|1605]]) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 16 (Old Calendar)|1=16th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839338}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Main Hall File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Secondary Torii File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Sub-shrine File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Sub-shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA)}} {{osm box|n|2462801468}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho [[commons:Category:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, Nara)]][[ja:皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)]] [[commons:Category:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, Nara)]][[ja:皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, Nara)]][[ja:皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48754918]] Wikidata name: Kotai Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称 = '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) |画像 = {{ill|File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|ja|File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|lt=270px|lt_ja=270px|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
皇大神社 |所在地 = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] Heimamatsuchō |祭神 = [[:en:Amaterasu|Great Sun Spirit]]
[[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] |社格 = Former Village shrine; offerings of sacred food, cloth, and ritual money{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = Built in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = [[:en:October 10|October 10]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city |image = File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg |location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.6791806|N|135.7792028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Heimamatsuchō, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. == Deities == * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] == Shrine Grounds == === Main Hall === Constructed in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]], bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “[[:en:Keichō|Keicho]] 10th year ([[:en:1605|1605]]) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 16 (Old Calendar)|1=16th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839338}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Main Hall File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Secondary Torii File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Sub-shrine File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Sub-shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA)}} {{osm box|n|2462801468}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)|version=82722734|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)]] [[ja:皇大神社]] {{Commons category|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q48754918}} {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane}}
{{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime}} |社格 = Former Village shrine; offerings of sacred food, cloth, and ritual money{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = Built in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = {{ill|October 10|en|October 10|WD=Q2921|lt=October 10}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city |image = File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg |location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.6791806|N|135.7792028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Heimamatsuchō, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime}} == Shrine Grounds == === Main Hall === Constructed in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Taisha}}, bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin}} {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite}}, which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “{{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keicho}} 10th year ({{ill|1605|en|1605|WD=Q6741|lt=1605}}) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 16 (Old Calendar)|1=16th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839338}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Main Hall File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Secondary Torii File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Sub-shrine File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Sub-shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)|version=82722734|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)]] [[ja:皇大神社]] {{Commons category|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q48754918}} [[Category:Completion It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Taisha}}, bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin}} {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite}}, which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “{{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keicho}} 10th year ({{ill|1605|en|1605|WD=Q6741|lt=1605}}) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 16 (Old Calendar)|1=16th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839338}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Main Hall File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Secondary Torii File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Sub-shrine File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Sub-shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)|version=82722734|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)]] [[ja:皇大神社]] {{Commons category|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q48754918}} [[Category:Completion {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime|1=Iwanaga-hime}} |社格 = Former Village shrine; offerings of sacred food, cloth, and ritual money{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = Built in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = {{ill|October 10|en|October 10|WD=Q2921|lt=October 10|1=October 10}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city |image = File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg |location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.6791806|N|135.7792028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Heimamatsuchō, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime|1=Iwanaga-hime}} == Shrine Grounds == === Main Hall === Constructed in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Taisha|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}}, which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “{{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keicho|1=Keichō}} 10th year ({{ill|1605|en|1605|WD=Q6741|lt=1605|1=1605}}) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 16 (Old Calendar)|1=March 16 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839338}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Main Hall File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Secondary Torii File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Sub-shrine File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Sub-shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA)}} {{osm box|n|2462801468}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)|version=82722734|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)]] [[ja:皇大神社]] {{Commons category|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q48754918}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Pages with files linked by ill]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata
