皇大神社 |所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}} Heimamatsuchō |祭神 = {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Great Sun Spirit|1=Amaterasu}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}
{{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime|1=Iwanaga-hime}} |社格 = Former Village shrine; offerings of sacred food, cloth, and ritual money{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=33}} |創建 = |本殿 = Built in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |例祭 = {{ill|October 10|en|October 10|WD=Q2921|lt=October 10|1=October 10}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 45.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 45.13 |地図 = Nara city |image = File:Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg |location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.6791806|N|135.7792028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kotai Jinja''' (皇大神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Heimamatsuchō, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime|1=Iwanaga-hime}} == Shrine Grounds == === Main Hall === Constructed in the Sankan-sha style with a copper-plated roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. It is said to be a relocation of the former main hall of the sessha Mizutani Shrine of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Taisha|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, bestowed upon Heimamatsu Sōdai Morita Heitarō—who served as the shrine woodmaster of Kasuga Taisha—due to that connection{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=911}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Sub-shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine – {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} {{ill|Kamitsutsunoo|ja|上筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} et al. They are enshrined on either side of the main hall, each housed in a small shrine with a vermilion-painted Kasuga-style cypress bark roof{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum Secondary Mound Remains === Within the shrine forest stands a massive andesite boulder {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}}, which is reputed to be the remnant of the secondary mound of the {{ill|Horaiyama Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|lt=Suinin Emperor's Mausoleum|lt_ja=垂仁天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. A shimenawa (sacred rope) is strung during the New Year{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. === Kannon Hall === To the west of the shrine grounds stands an area known as Kannon Hall, where old roof tiles and other relics have been discovered{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Shrine Treasures == === Kagura Bells === An incised inscription reading “{{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keicho|1=Keichō}} 10th year ({{ill|1605|en|1605|WD=Q6741|lt=1605|1=1605}}) {{ill|March 16 (Old Calendar)|ja|3月16日 (旧暦)|lt=March Sixteen|lt_ja=三月十六日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 16 (Old Calendar)|1=March 16 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839338}} Hiramatsu Uemon” is present{{sfn|奈良市史|p=412}}. == Gallery == File:Kotai Jinja Honden (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Main Hall File:Kotai Jinja Keidai (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Secondary Torii File:Kotai Jinja Komainu A (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Komainu N (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Komainu File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 1 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Right Sub-shrine File:Kotai Jinja Wakiden 2 (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA).jpg|Left Sub-shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, NARA)}} {{osm box|n|2462801468}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 8: Architecture|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Edition|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)|version=82722734|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:皇大神社 (奈良市平松町)]] [[ja:皇大神社]] {{Commons category|Kotai Jinja (Hiramatsu-cho, Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q48754918}}
